evals.name: new-adr-happy-path
evals.prompt: Record an ADR for our team. We decided to adopt PostgreSQL as the primary datastore. Context: we were on SQLite and hit concurrency limits during the last load test (450 concurrent writes, lock contention killed throughput). Decision: migrate to PostgreSQL 16 on managed RDS. Alternatives: MySQL 8 (team has no operational experience), CockroachDB (overkill for our scale, +40% cost), stay on SQLite (already proven to fail). Consequences: positive — concurrency headroom, mature tooling; negative — ops complexity, monthly bill +$120; neutral — supersedes our 'SQLite forever' decision in ADR-0003.
evals.expected_output: A new ADR file at <repo>/docs/adr/NNNN-postgresql-primary-datastore.md (or similar slug) with all 6 sections filled. The ADR README.md index gets a new row. ADR-0003 should NOT be modified by this run (supersede chain is a separate step per skill boundaries).

assertions.text: File exists at docs/adr/NNNN-*.md with NNNN matching the next sequence number
File contains all 6 section headings (## Status / ## Date / ## Context / ## Decision / ## Alternatives Considered / ## Consequences)
Decision section uses imperative voice (contains 'Adopt' or 'Migrate' or 'Replace', not 'We should')
Alternatives Considered has at least 3 labeled blocks (MySQL, CockroachDB, SQLite)
Consequences has Positive / Negative / Neutral subsections
ADR README.md index gained exactly 1 new row

evals.name: missing-adr-directory
evals.prompt: Let's ADR this: we're going to use Tailwind for styling because our designer wants utility classes. Alternatives: CSS modules (too much boilerplate). Consequences: positive — faster iteration; negative — purge config maintenance.
evals.expected_output: Repo has no docs/adr/ directory. The skill should ASK once before creating it. If the user confirms, create docs/adr/ with a README.md index template, then write ADR-0001. If declined, stop with a clear message.

assertions.text: Skill asks user before creating docs/adr/
If created, docs/adr/README.md contains the index table header and template note
The first ADR written is numbered 0001 (zero-padded)
If user declined creation, no files are written and a clear refusal message is shown

evals.name: sequence-collision-and-incomplete-input
evals.prompt: Write ADR-0010 about our decision to switch from npm to pnpm.
evals.expected_output: Two distinct concerns to test: (a) the user only supplied a Decision (no Context, no Alternatives, no Consequences) — the agent must refuse with a 'needs more input' block and not write a partial ADR. (b) If we pre-populate the directory with 0010-*.md before running, the skill must detect the collision and refuse to overwrite — it should suggest the next available number instead.

assertions.text: Skill identifies missing sections (Context, Alternatives, Consequences) and asks for them before any file write
No file is created when sections are missing
When ADR-0010 already exists, skill detects collision and proposes next available number (e.g., 0011)
